passage.title: Helping Hands Trial
passage.text: Our community club tested two ways to sort food-drive donations last month. Hana used one open table pile, but Evan used short shelf labels. After a busy drive week, Evan's team finished the job more calmly because the shelf labels helped the team place items quickly. The club counted each donation and checked where it was placed. When Hana saw the difference, she changed to short shelf labels. After more work, her team placed items in the right bins more often. The students talked about the test before choosing a better method. The group learned that easy labels can make helping work more organized.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mostly about?
choices: Finding an easier sorting method for community work
questions.rationale: The whole passage follows one test and what the group learned from it.

questions.prompt: What did Evan's team use to sort the donations?
questions.rationale: The second sentence directly tells which tool or place was used.

questions.prompt: What did the group check or write down?
questions.rationale: The fourth sentence explains what the group checked or recorded.

questions.prompt: What does difference mean in the fifth sentence?
choices: A loud sound in a room
The way two things are not the same
questions.rationale: Difference means the two results did not match each other.

questions.prompt: What does donation mean in the first sentence?
choices: A person who visits a club
questions.rationale: The word meaning comes from how it is used in the passage.

questions.prompt: Why did Hana switch to short shelf labels?
choices: The first tool was missing from the class
She thought shelf labels would reduce sorting mistakes
The teacher told the student to stop working
The group wanted to decorate the room more
questions.rationale: The student changed after seeing which method worked better.
